DISCOVERY 525, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND OF NONAPPEARANCE CONTINUED FROM OCT-08-2024 TO DISCOVERY AT OCT-29-2024, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 525 ON COURTS OWN MOTION. = (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE OCT-29-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND OF NONAPPEARANCE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED GRANTED. NO TIMELY OPPOSITION OR PROOF OF SERVICE OF SUBSTANTIALLY CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES. CCP 2033.280. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (525/JPT)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Douglas Agne, Esq. 760-842-5850 Reese Law Group for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Of Nonappearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The Agne Declaration does not state that the parties met and conferred in person, by telephone, or via video conference in compliance with CCP 439. The parties are ordered to meet and confer.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Oct-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DOUGLAS AGNE, Esq. of Reese Law Group (tel: 760-842-5850) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ONEMAIN FINANCIAL GROUP's Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to deem admitted GRANTED. No timely opposition or proof of service of substantially code-compliant responses. CCP 2033.280.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email to the Judge Pro Tem.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
